questions.ai: Are Bluetooth / BLE devices prohibited at any customer sites? This is critical for device selection.
questions.learnedFrom: Schneider Electric kickoff — BLE was strictly prohibited at all sites, which had major device selection implications

questions.ai: Do any shipments have unusually long dwell times (over 12 weeks)? This impacts battery life planning.
options: Yes — some shipments dwell for months
questions.learnedFrom: Schneider — equipment may dwell at e-house builders for 20–22 weeks; battery life became a critical factor

questions.ai: Do you need to share tracking visibility with third parties (truckers, carriers, partners) who don't have portal access?
questions.learnedFrom: Multiple customers needed public tracking links to share with carriers who didn't have Decklar accounts

questions.ai: Have you confirmed all devices are within 9 months of expiry? This affects inventory readiness.
options: ❌ Some devices are expired / close to expiry
questions.learnedFrom: Standard onboarding best practice — expired devices cause shipment delays

questions.ai: What does success look like for this deployment? Be specific — what problem are we solving and how will you measure it?
questions.learnedFrom: Schneider — success criteria revealed the real business driver was moisture intrusion claims, not just tracking

questions.ai: What is the expected geofence radius at origin and destination facilities? This affects trigger sensitivity.
questions.learnedFrom: Geofence radius directly impacts when shipment creation and completion triggers fire
